all.title: AI predicts wildfire risk faster than current warning systems, new study finds
all.description: A wildfire forecasting system powered by artificial intelligence was around 30% better at identifying dangerous fire conditions in advance than the models currently used, according to new research. The Australian and New Zealand researchers’ study, published in the International Journal of Wildland Fire, trained a machine learning model on historical weather and fire data from three […

all.title: North America: Hot, dry winter may bring a burning spring
all.description: As we come into the north-flowing warmth of the North American spring, the impacts of snow drought and heat dome are prepping wildland fuels for what may be a transition to a “super” El Nino. Yet it is spring, and reading the North American Seasonal Fire Assessment and Outlook for April through June we note […]

all.title: Wildland firefighters may soon get hazard pay during prescribed fires in the US
all.description: Federal officials are considering a rule change that would make wildland firefighters conducting prescribed fires eligible for hazardous duty pay. The Office of Personnel Management recently posted the proposed rule on the Federal Register, which specified that prescribed burns conducted by General Schedule employees would trigger Hazardous Duty Pay, while Federal Wage System employees would [&#82

all.title: Is the US Forest Service’s reorganization a ‘dismantling’ or a needed shift? Experts weigh in on wildland fire’s impact
all.description: The United States Department of Agriculture’s recent announcement to make sweeping structural changes to the nation’s Forest Service has brought confusion and uncertainty countrywide, ahead of what’s expected to be a very active wildfire year. all.title: The smell of danger – understanding wildfire smoke exposure
all.description: By Anthony White At a laboratory in Australia, researchers are trying to develop rapid, accessible tests that can protect firefighters, military personnel, and the wider community from the escalating health burden of wildfire smoke and polluted air. The olfactory tissue, located high in the nasal cavity, directly connects the outside world to the brain, making […]

all.title: User feedback on the Interagency Fuel Treatment Decision Support System
all.description: By Josh Hyde, Eva Strand, Peter Noble and Kim Ernstrom Land and fire managers are often tasked to plan complex fuel treatments and apply the best available science across jurisdictions to prioritize, plan, and conduct prescribed burns and other fire mitigation tactics. all.title: Europe’s 2025 wildfire season was its worst ever, by far
all.description: Wildfires burned over 2.7 million acres (1.1 million hectares) across the European Union in 2025, by far the highest burned area total recorded since record keeping began. The EU’s Joint Research Centre recently released its 2025 analysis wildfires, which found the historic season started early in March, with over 379,000 acres (153,000 hectares) burning. Intensity […]

all.title: Forest Service to undergo ‘sweeping restructuring,’ close all regional offices
all.description: The United States Department of Agriculture this week announced the nation’s Forest Service will soon go through massive changes, including the shift of its headquarters across the country. The agency’s headquarters will move from Washington D.C. to Salt Lake City, Utah, according to a press release sent out on Tuesday. The agency will also close […]
